---
title: "EDA"
author: "Israa Atike"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```



```{r}

library(readr)

data <- read_csv("C:/Users/Israa Atike/Documents/Project/thesis-project-israa-atike/data/processed_data.csv")

head(data)
```
```{r}
str(data)
```
```{r}
# Load libraries
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)

categorical_summary <- function(df, col_name) {
  summary_table <- df %>%
    group_by(.data[[col_name]]) %>%
    reframe(
      Count = n(),
      Percentage = round(100 * n() / nrow(df), 2)
    ) %>%
    ungroup() %>%                  # make sure it's ungrouped
    arrange(desc(Count))
  
  print(
    knitr::kable(
      summary_table,
      caption = paste("Table: Distribution of", col_name),
      col.names = c(col_name, "Count", "Percentage (%)"),
      align = "lcc"
    )
  )
}

# List of categorical variables 

categorical_vars <- c(
  "race", "gender", "age", "readmitted", 
  "change", "diabetesMed", 
  "diag_1", "diag_2", "diag_3",
  "max_glu_serum", "A1Cresult",
  "metformin", "repaglinide", "nateglinide", "chlorpropamide",
  "glimepiride", "acetohexamide", "glipizide", "glyburide", "tolbutamide",
  "pioglitazone", "rosiglitazone", "acarbose", "miglitol", "troglitazone",
  "tolazamide", "insulin",
  "glyburide-metformin", "glipizide-metformin",
  "metformin-pioglitazone"
)

# Generate tables for all categorical variables
for (var in categorical_vars) {
  categorical_summary(data, var)
}


```
```{r}
# Load libraries
library(tidyverse)

# List of categorical columns
categorical_cols <- c(
  "gender", "race", "age",
  "admission_type_id", "discharge_disposition_id", "admission_source_id",
  "diag_1", "diag_2", "diag_3",
  "metformin", "repaglinide", "nateglinide", "chlorpropamide",
  "glimepiride", "acetohexamide", "glipizide", "glyburide", "tolbutamide",
  "pioglitazone", "rosiglitazone", "acarbose", "miglitol", "troglitazone",
  "tolazamide", "insulin",
  "glyburide-metformin", "glipizide-metformin",
  "metformin-pioglitazone",
  "change", "diabetesMed", "readmitted"
)

#  barplots for all categorical variables
for (col in categorical_cols) {
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[col]])) +
    geom_bar(
      fill = "#4C72B0",       
      color = "grey30",      
      width = 0.65
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(
        angle = 45, 
        hjust = 1, 
        color = "grey20"
      ),
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20"),
      axis.title = element_text(color = "grey20", face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(
        face = "bold", 
        hjust = 0.5,
        color = "grey10"
      ),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(), 
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Distribution of", col),
      x = col,
      y = "Count of Patients"
    )
  
  print(p)
}

```

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# List of numeric columns
numeric_cols <- c(
  "time_in_hospital",
  "num_lab_procedures",
  "num_procedures",
  "num_medications",
  "number_outpatient",
  "number_emergency",
  "number_inpatient",
  "number_diagnoses"
)

for (col in numeric_cols) {

  # Dynamic bin width
  range_val <- max(data[[col]], na.rm = TRUE) - min(data[[col]], na.rm = TRUE)
  bin_width <- ceiling(range_val / 30)

  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[col]])) +
    geom_histogram(
      binwidth = bin_width,
      fill = "#4C72B0",  
      color = "grey30", 
      alpha = 0.9
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Distribution of", col),
      x = col,
      y = "Frequency"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(color = "grey20"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20"),
      axis.title = element_text(color = "grey20", face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(
        face = "bold",
        hjust = 0.5,
        color = "grey10"
      ),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )

  print(p)
}

```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)

numeric_cols <- c(
  "time_in_hospital",
  "num_lab_procedures",
  "num_procedures",
  "num_medications",
  "number_outpatient",
  "number_emergency",
  "number_inpatient",
  "number_diagnoses"
)

for (col in numeric_cols) {
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = readmitted, y = .data[[col]], fill = readmitted)) +
    geom_boxplot(
      color = "grey30",
      width = 0.6,
      outlier.color = "grey50"
    ) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(
        "NO"  = "#4C72B0",  
        ">30" = "#55A868",  
        "<30" = "#DD8452"   #
      )
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(color = "grey20"),
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20"),
      axis.title = element_text(color = "grey20", face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(
        face = "bold",
        hjust = 0.5,
        color = "grey10"
      ),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "none"
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Boxplot of", col, "by Readmission Status"),
      x = "Readmission Status",
      y = col
    )

  print(p)
}


```












```{r}
# Select numerical variables only (
num_df <- data[, c(
  "time_in_hospital",
  "num_lab_procedures",
  "num_procedures",
  "num_medications",
  "number_outpatient",
  "number_emergency",
  "number_inpatient",
  "number_diagnoses"
)]

# Scatterplot matrix
pairs(
  num_df,
  main = "Scatterplot Matrix of Numerical Features",
  pch = 19,        
  col = "#4C72B0AA",  
  cex = 0.6        
)


```
```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(ggplot2)
library(reshape2) 

numeric_cols <- c(
  "time_in_hospital",
  "num_lab_procedures",
  "num_procedures",
  "num_medications",
  "number_outpatient",
  "number_emergency",
  "number_inpatient",
  "number_diagnoses"
)

cor_matrix <- cor(data[, numeric_cols], use = "complete.obs")

melted_cor <- melt(cor_matrix)

# Correlation heatmap
ggplot(melted_cor, aes(x = Var1, y = Var2, fill = value)) +
  geom_tile(color = "grey90", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#4C72B0",    
    mid = "white",
    high = "#DD8452",   
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1)
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = sprintf("%.2f", value)),
    size = 3,
    color = "grey20"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "Correlation Heatmap of Numerical Features",
    x = "",
    y = "",
    fill = "Correlation"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(
      angle = 45,
      hjust = 1,
      color = "grey20",
      face = "bold"
    ),
    axis.text.y = element_text(
      color = "grey20",
      face = "bold"
    ),
    plot.title = element_text(
      hjust = 0.5,
      face = "bold",
      color = "grey10"
    ),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

```


```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)

# All categorical variables
categorical_cols <- c(
  "gender", "race", "age",
  "admission_type_id", "discharge_disposition_id", "admission_source_id",
  "diag_1", "diag_2", "diag_3",
  "metformin", "repaglinide", "nateglinide", "chlorpropamide",
  "glimepiride", "acetohexamide", "glipizide", "glyburide", "tolbutamide",
  "pioglitazone", "rosiglitazone", "acarbose", "miglitol", "troglitazone",
  "tolazamide", "insulin",
  "glyburide-metformin", "glipizide-metformin", 
  "metformin-pioglitazone",
  "change", "diabetesMed"
)

# Remove near-zero variance variables
nzv_vars <- c(
  "nateglinide", "chlorpropamide", "acetohexamide", "tolbutamide",
  "acarbose", "miglitol", "troglitazone", "tolazamide",
  "glyburide-metformin", "glipizide-metformin",
   "metformin-pioglitazone"
)

categorical_cols <- setdiff(categorical_cols, nzv_vars)

# Function to create pivoted table for a categorical variable
categorical_table <- function(df, cat_var) {
  df %>%
    group_by(.data[[cat_var]], readmitted) %>%
    summarise(Count = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    pivot_wider(
      names_from = readmitted,
      values_from = Count,
      values_fill = 0
    ) %>%
    arrange(.data[[cat_var]]) %>%
    rename(!!cat_var := 1) %>%
    kable(caption = paste("Distribution of", cat_var, "by Readmission"))
}

# Loop over all selected categorical variables
for (var in categorical_cols) {
  print(categorical_table(data, var))
}





```


```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(reshape2)
library(scales)


# All categorical columns
categorical_cols <- c(
  "gender", "race", "age",
  "admission_type_id", "discharge_disposition_id", "admission_source_id",
  "diag_1", "diag_2", "diag_3",
  "metformin", "repaglinide",
  "glimepiride", "glipizide", "glyburide", "pioglitazone", "rosiglitazone",
  "insulin", "change", "diabetesMed"
)

# Near-zero variance vars to remove
nzv_vars <- c(
  "nateglinide", "chlorpropamide", "acetohexamide", "tolbutamide",
  "acarbose", "miglitol", "troglitazone", "tolazamide",
  "glyburide-metformin", "glipizide-metformin", "metformin-pioglitazone"
)

categorical_cols <- setdiff(categorical_cols, nzv_vars)
data$readmitted <- factor(
  data$readmitted,
  levels = c("No", ">30", "<30")
)

# Barplots for remaining categorical variables
for (col in categorical_cols) {
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = .data[[col]], fill = readmitted)) +
    geom_bar(
      position = "fill",
      color = "grey30",
      width = 0.75
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
    scale_fill_manual(
      values = c(
        "No"  = "#4C72B0", 
        ">30" = "#55A868",  
        "<30" = "#DD8452"   
      )
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      axis.text.x = element_text(
        angle = 45,
        hjust = 1,
        color = "grey20"
      ),
      axis.text.y = element_text(color = "grey20"),
      axis.title = element_text(color = "grey20", face = "bold"),
      legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      plot.title = element_text(
        face = "bold",
        hjust = 0.5,
        color = "grey10"
      ),
      panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Readmission Distribution by", col),
      x = col,
      y = "Percentage",
      fill = "Readmitted"
    )

  print(p)
}

```




```{r}

library(dplyr)



numeric_cols <- c(

  "time_in_hospital",

  "num_lab_procedures",

  "num_procedures",

  "num_medications",

  "number_outpatient",

  "number_emergency",

  "number_inpatient",

  "number_diagnoses"

)



numeric_summary_by_readmitted <- data %>%

  group_by(readmitted) %>%

  summarise(across(all_of(numeric_cols), 

                   list(mean = ~mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE),

                        median = ~median(.x, na.rm = TRUE),

                        min = ~min(.x, na.rm = TRUE),

                        max = ~max(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),

                   .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"),

            .groups = "drop")



print(numeric_summary_by_readmitted)



numeric_summary_overall <- data %>%

  summarise(across(all_of(numeric_cols),

                   list(mean = ~round(mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE), 2),

                        median = ~median(.x, na.rm = TRUE),

                        min = ~min(.x, na.rm = TRUE),

                        max = ~max(.x, na.rm = TRUE)),

                   .names = "{.col}_{.fn}"))



print(numeric_summary_overall)





```
